name: Archon2
description: Sibling variant of Archon1 with superior membrane localization and brightness at the cost of reduced sensitivity.

spectrum.name: Archon2
spectrum.note: Emission: borrowed from the near-identical sibling Archon1, digitized from Song et al. 2024 (doi:10.1117/1.NPh.11.2.024201, Fig. 1b) — the first reported Archon1 emission spectrum (638 nm excitation, N=3 cells), peak ~693 nm. Excitation: a Gaussian MODEL centered on Archon's absorption maximum (586 nm; measured for Archon2 by Penzkofer et al. 2021, doi:10.1016/j.jphotobiol.2021.112331). The only published Archon absorption (Penzkofer Fig. 1a) is a 15-curve photocycle-degradation series with the 'gLED 590 nm' reference spike drawn over the 586 nm peak and the inset/annotation covering the blue flank, so it cannot be cleanly digitized — the excitation curve is therefore modeled (shown dashed), not measured.

voltage.name: Archon2
voltage.note: F-V (ΔF/F vs membrane potential) from the Archon2 population data in Suppl. Fig. 16c (n=5 neurons from 3 cultures; 637 nm laser, 800 mW/mm², 2.3 kHz). Near-linear, ΔF/F=0 at −70 mV, rising to ~+21% at +40 mV; the −70→+30 mV (100-mV) step ≈ +19–20%, consistent with the 19±2% in Suppl. Table 1 and 20% in Fig. 1g. Fig. 2 in the main paper characterizes Archon1 only, so the F-V curve comes from Suppl. Fig. 16c — previously mis-labeled 'Fig. 2e' with a source image byte-identical to Archon1's Fig. 2d/e (now replaced with the correct Fig. 16c crop).

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean τ of the bi-exponential fit F(t)=A(C·exp(-t/τfast)+(1-C)·exp(-t/τslow)), neuronal culture at 32 °C (n = 9 neurons). On: τfast 0.06 ms (70%) + τslow 6.7 ms (30%) → 2.05 ms. Off: τfast 0.17 ms (92%) + τslow 7.0 ms (8%) → 0.72 ms. Previously stored the fast components (0.06/0.17) alone; component amplitudes (C) are reported in Table S1.

sensitivityData.note: In-vivo ΔF/F per action potential = 9.4 ± 2.1% in mouse motor cortex layer 2/3 pyramidal neurons/interneurons (CAG / in utero electroporation), widefield 1P at 1.5 W/mm², 1 kHz (Suppl. Table 2). The per-100-mV in-vivo value in the same row is 18.8 ± 8.3% (this is the per-AP column). Archon2's per-AP response (9.4%) is about half its per-100-mV response, consistent with its slower kinetics capturing less of a brief spike.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, cultured hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Optical single-AP FWHM ≈ 2.4 ms, derived by digitizing the blue optical spikes in Suppl. Fig. 16e (three single, non-averaged APs measured at 2.37 / 2.39 / 2.95 ms against the 10-ms scale bar; median ~2.4 ms). Cultured mouse hippocampal neurons, widefield 1P (637 nm, 800 mW/mm²), image acquisition 3.2 kHz. Approximate — these are individual (un-averaged) noisy APs, unlike Archon1's quantified n=160 Fig. 2h dataset. Recording temperature not specified.

brightnessData.note: Molecular brightness relative to Arch (Monakhov et al. 2020, nirButterfly paper, Table 1: Archon2 = 80×, Archon1 = 28×). Corrected from a prior value of 28, which is Archon1's row — the Archon1/Archon2 vs-Arch brightness values were swapped. Cross-checked by footnote d (Archon2 is 8× brighter than QuasAr2, Archon1 is 2.8× brighter), consistent with the Fig. 2b entry on this page (archon2 = 8.01× quasar2).

photostabilityData.note: Read from the blue Archon2 curve in Fig. 2i: fluorescence falls to ~55% of initial after 900 s (15 min) of continuous 637-nm illumination at 800 mW/mm² in cultured neurons. The prior stored value (98%) was a misread of this panel — the blue Archon2 trace clearly ends near 55%, far below the magenta Archon1 trace (~92-95%, which matches Archon1's stored 95%). The paper otherwise reports Archon2 photobleaching only as a rate (0.03%/s, Suppl. Table 1).

photobleach.note: Blue Archon2 curve, 637 nm 800 mW/mm² (n=7 neurons); retains ~59% at 900 s, t75≈534 s. Origin-paper photobleaching panel Fig. 2i, cultured mouse hippocampal neurons, continuous illumination. IMPORTANT: the five sensors in this panel were each imaged at a DIFFERENT intensity/wavelength (Ace2N-4aa-mNeon 13 mW/mm² 475 nm; QuasAr2 & Archer1 2200 mW/mm² 637 nm; Archon1 & Archon2 800 mW/mm² 637 nm), so the raw F/F0 cross-overlay (shared source+Fig. 2i) is NOT iso-condition — it reproduces exactly what the paper plotted. intensityMWmm2 is intentionally OMITTED (linear dose-scaling across a 13-2200 mW/mm² spread would give misleading values), so each t75 stands at its native intensity.
